title: silene inflata subsp. puberula
intro: Silene inflata subsp. puberula, also known as the inflated catchfly or puberula catchfly, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family, characterized by its pinkish-purple flowers and inflated seed capsules. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Silene inflata subsp. puberula include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried flower or root infusion, or as a tincture, and is typically combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
